div.p: Write from Process A → Process B with barrier synchronization points

Key Insight: Without barriers, steps 2-6 may be reordered by CPU. The RELEASE barrier ensures the store buffer drains before the head index update. The ACQUIRE barrier ensures invalidation queue is processed before reading message data.

Step 1: Store issued ~1 cycle Step 2: L1 commit ~4 cycles Step 3-4: L1→L2→L3 ~20-40 cycles Step 5: Invalidate ~40-80 cycles Step 6: DRAM write ~100-300 cycles Step 7-8: DRAM read ~100-300 cycles Step 9: Load complete ~4 cycles Total: 200-700+ cycles (depends on cache state)
